articles.title: Iran threatens OpenAI’s Stargate data center in Abu Dhabi
articles.summary: Iran's IRGC released a video threatening OpenAI's $30 billion Stargate data center in Abu Dhabi as retaliation if the US attacks Iranian power plants.

articles.title: Cisco CEO Chuck Robbins wants data centers in space
articles.summary: Cisco CEO Chuck Robbins discusses plans to establish data centers in space. The company aims to expand its networking infrastructure beyond Earth to support future computing needs.

articles.title: Suno is a music copyright nightmare
articles.summary: Suno's AI music platform claims to prevent copyrighted material use, but its copyright filters are easily bypassed. Users can generate AI imitations of popular songs with minimal effort using free software.

articles.title: AI startup Rocket offers vibe McKinsey-style reports at a fraction of the cost
articles.summary: Rocket's AI platform delivers McKinsey-style strategy reports at lower costs, expanding beyond code generation to include product building and competitive intelligence.

articles.title: OpenAI alums have been quietly investing from a new, potentially $100M fund
articles.summary: Zero Shot, a venture capital fund led by OpenAI alumni, is raising up to $100 million for its first fund and has already begun making investments.

articles.title: Google quietly launched an AI dictation app that works offline
articles.summary: Google launched an offline-first AI dictation app using Gemma models, competing with apps like Whisper Flow. The app functions without internet connection.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway, a San Francisco cloud platform with 2 million developers, raised $100 million Series B to build AI-native infrastructure challenging AWS and Google Cloud amid growing AI application demands.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code's $20-$200 monthly pricing has frustrated developers, leading them to adopt Goose, a free open-source alternative by Block offering similar AI coding capabilities without subscription fees.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B after a viral billboard hiring campaign featuring AI token puzzles. The creative stunt decoded to a coding challenge, attracting thousands of applicants and demonstrating innovative recruitment.

articles.title: OpenClaw gives users yet another reason to be freaked out about security
articles.summary: OpenClaw, a popular AI agentic tool with 347,000 GitHub stars, poses security risks due to its broad system access. A recently patched vulnerability highlights dangers of granting such extensive permissions to autonomous AI agents.

articles.title: New Rowhammer attacks give complete control of machines running Nvidia GPUs
articles.summary: Researchers discovered three novel Rowhammer attacks exploiting memory vulnerabilities in Nvidia GPUs, allowing malicious users in cloud environments to gain complete root control of shared machines.

articles.title: Quantum computers need vastly fewer resources than thought to break vital encryption
articles.summary: Recent whitepapers show quantum computers need far fewer resources than expected to break elliptic-curve encryption, with neutral atoms achieving 256-bit ECC breaking in 10 days using 100x less overhead.

articles.title: The one piece of data that could actually shed light on your job and AI
articles.summary: Silicon Valley leaders predict AI will significantly disrupt jobs, with some suggesting AI could replace human labor across all sectors within five years, potentially causing recessions and breaking entry-level career pathways.

articles.title: AI is changing how small online sellers decide what to make
articles.summary: Small online sellers increasingly use AI tools like Alibaba's Accio to find manufacturers and decide what products to make, replacing traditionally slow manual research processes.

articles.title: Four things we’d need to put data centers in space
articles.summary: SpaceX filed an FCC application to launch up to one million orbital data centers to support AI growth without environmental impact on Earth. Tech giants like Amazon and Google are also exploring space-based computing infrastructure.

articles.title: Welcome Gemma 4: Frontier multimodal intelligence on device
articles.summary: Google introduces Gemma 4, a frontier multimodal AI model designed for on-device deployment. It combines advanced intelligence capabilities with efficient performance for local processing.

articles.title: Holo3: Breaking the Computer Use Frontier
articles.summary: Holo3 represents a breakthrough in computer use AI, advancing how artificial intelligence interacts with digital systems and applications.

articles.title: Falcon Perception
articles.summary: Falcon Perception appears to be an AI system focused on visual understanding and perception capabilities. The article lacks detailed content for a complete summary.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Kevin Scott discusses future directions in AI development and innovation. The conversation explores upcoming advancements and trends shaping the AI landscape ahead.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Brands like Hot Wheels are leveraging Microsoft AI tools to boost productivity and creativity in content creation and business operations.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft released an open-source toolkit for future farming, enabling developers to build agricultural solutions with AI technology.

articles.title: New ways to balance cost and reliability in the Gemini API
articles.summary: Google introduces Flex and Priority inference tiers for the Gemini API to balance cost and latency tradeoffs for developers.

articles.title: Create, edit and share videos at no cost in Google Vids
articles.summary: Google Vids introduces free AI-powered video creation and editing features using Lyria 3 and Veo 3.1 technology, enabling users to generate high-quality videos at no cost.

articles.title: We’re creating a new satellite imagery map to help protect Brazil’s forests.
articles.summary: Google partnered with the Brazilian government to create a satellite imagery map designed to protect Brazil's forests. The technology enables better monitoring and conservation efforts.

articles.title: Gemma 4: Byte for byte, the most capable open models
articles.summary: Google released Gemma 4, its most capable open models yet, designed specifically for advanced reasoning and agentic workflows.

articles.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash Live: Making audio AI more natural and reliable
articles.summary: Gemini 3.1 Flash Live improves voice AI with better precision and lower latency, enabling more natural and fluid audio interactions.

articles.title: Protecting people from harmful manipulation
articles.summary: Google DeepMind researches AI's harmful manipulation risks in finance and health sectors, implementing new safety measures to protect people.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #238 - GPT 5.4 mini, OpenAI Pivot, Mamba 3, Attention Residuals
articles.summary: OpenAI released GPT-5.4 mini and nano models with 400k-token context windows and improved token efficiency. The episode discusses these releases alongside Mamba 3 and attention residual developments in AI research.

articles.title: Last Week in AI #339 - DLSS 5, OpenAI Superapp, MiniMax M2.7
articles.summary: Nvidia launched DLSS 5, using generative AI to enhance video game graphics in real-time up to 4K by analyzing scene semantics and lighting. The technology blends traditional rendering with AI for photorealistic results.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #237 - Nemotron 3 Super, xAI reborn, Anthropic Lawsuit, Research!
articles.summary: Episode 237 covers major AI developments including Perplexity's local Mac-based AI agent, Anthropic's GitHub PR review pricing, and new interactive visualization features from ChatGPT and Anthropic.

articles.title: As AI agents take on more tasks, governance becomes a priority
articles.summary: As AI agents evolve from simple response systems to autonomous decision-makers, governance frameworks become critical. Organizations must establish clear boundaries, access controls, and tracking mechanisms to safely manage these autonomous systems.

articles.title: KiloClaw targets shadow AI with autonomous agent governance
articles.summary: KiloClaw is an enterprise platform that manages shadow AI by providing governance over autonomous agents deployed outside official channels. It addresses the BYOAI trend where employees bypass procurement to automate workflows, exposing proprietary data to unregulated environments.

articles.title: 5 best practices to secure AI systems
articles.summary: AI systems require multi-layered security strategies including data protection, access control, and monitoring. Key practices include strict access governance, role-based permissions, and encryption to protect sensitive models.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from PSU and Duke developed methods to automatically identify which agent causes task failures in LLM multi-agent systems and when failures occur, addressing debugging challenges in collaborative AI systems.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance introduced Astra, a dual-model architecture for autonomous robot navigation that addresses localization, destination identification, and path planning challenges in complex indoor environments.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to self-improve by generating training data through self-editing and updating their own weights via reinforcement learning.

articles.title: Why harness engineering is becoming the new AI moat
articles.summary: As large language models become commoditized, sophisticated engineering systems surrounding them are emerging as the true competitive advantage for AI companies.

articles.title: How GhostClaw malware targets the OpenClaw AI agent boom
articles.summary: GhostClaw malware exploits macOS binaries to steal credentials from developers running local OpenClaw AI agents on Mac Minis, capitalizing on the growing trend of local AI deployment.

articles.title: Why Meta’s V-JEPA 2.1 model is a massive step forward for real-world AI
articles.summary: Meta's V-JEPA 2.1 advances video self-supervised learning by balancing motion tracking with spatial detail, overcoming historical limitations in AI models.

articles.title: Meta AI Releases EUPE: A Compact Vision Encoder Family Under 100M Parameters That Rivals Specialist Models Across Image Understanding, Dense Prediction, and VLM Tasks
articles.summary: Meta AI introduced EUPE, a compact vision encoder under 100M parameters that efficiently handles diverse vision tasks like image classification and segmentation simultaneously without sacrificing performance on edge devices.

articles.title: An Implementation Guide to Running NVIDIA Transformer Engine with Mixed Precision, FP8 Checks, Benchmarking, and Fallback Execution
articles.summary: Tutorial on implementing NVIDIA Transformer Engine with mixed-precision acceleration in Python, comparing baseline PyTorch with optimized paths through benchmarking and performance analysis.

articles.title: RightNow AI Releases AutoKernel: An Open-Source Framework that Applies an Autonomous Agent Loop to GPU Kernel Optimization for Arbitrary PyTorch Models
articles.summary: RightNow AI released AutoKernel, an open-source framework using autonomous LLM agents to automatically optimize GPU kernels for PyTorch models, eliminating the need for GPU expertise.

articles.title: LG G6 vs. Samsung S95H: I compared the best OLED TVs of 2026 and made a tough choice
articles.summary: Comparing two premium 2026 OLED TVs: LG G6 and Samsung S95H. Both offer excellent picture and sound quality, with the choice depending on individual preferences and budget.

articles.title: I found Android Auto's hidden shortcut that automates any task in your car - and it's brilliant
articles.summary: Android Auto has a hidden Custom Assistant feature that automates car tasks with minimal setup. This overlooked functionality offers significant convenience for drivers.

articles.title: Samsung's latest TV firmware update fixes the Chromecast issue for older models - finally
articles.summary: Samsung's latest firmware update adds Google Cast support to older TV models dating back to 2024, resolving previous Chromecast compatibility issues across their lineup.

articles.title: Top 5 Reranking Models to Improve RAG Results
articles.summary: The article discusses five reranking models that enhance retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) systems by improving result quality and relevance in information retrieval tasks.

articles.title: 7 Machine Learning Trends to Watch in 2026
articles.summary: Machine learning systems are evolving beyond backend dashboards into more visible and integrated applications. Key trends for 2026 include advances in ML deployment, accessibility, and real-world integration.

articles.title: Building a ‘Human-in-the-Loop’ Approval Gate for Autonomous Agents
articles.summary: The article discusses state-managed interruptions in agentic AI systems, where execution pipelines are intentionally halted to implement human oversight. This creates approval gates ensuring human control over autonomous agent decisions.

articles.title: The Geometry Behind the Dot Product: Unit Vectors, Projections, and Intuition
articles.summary: An article exploring the geometric foundations of the dot product, covering unit vectors, projections, and intuitive understanding of this fundamental mathematical concept.

articles.title: How to Run Claude Code Agents in Parallel
articles.summary: Learn techniques to run Claude code agents in parallel for improved efficiency and faster task completion.

articles.title: Behavior is the New Credential
articles.summary: Authentication is shifting from knowledge-based and biometric methods to behavioral analysis. Users are now verified through how they act online rather than what they know or look like.

articles.title: National Robotics Week — Latest Physical AI Research, Breakthroughs and Resources
articles.summary: National Robotics Week highlights AI breakthroughs bringing robots into the physical world across industries. Advancements in robot learning, simulation, and foundation models accelerate real-world deployment.

articles.title: From RTX to Spark: NVIDIA Accelerates Gemma 4 for Local Agentic AI
articles.summary: Google and NVIDIA optimized Gemma 4 models for local AI execution across devices, from RTX PCs to data centers. These small, efficient models enable on-device agentic AI with real-time context processing.

articles.title: Press Start on April: GeForce NOW Brings 10 Games to the Cloud
articles.summary: GeForce NOW launches 10 new games in April, including Capcom's PRAGMATA and Arknights: Endfield 3D strategy game. The cloud gaming service continues expanding its library with fresh titles.

articles.title: Build AI-powered employee onboarding agents with Amazon Quick
articles.summary: Amazon Quick enables HR departments to build no-code AI agents that automate employee onboarding by answering questions, tracking compliance, and reducing manual tasks to accelerate new hire productivity.

articles.title: Accelerate agentic tool calling with serverless model customization in Amazon SageMaker AI
articles.summary: Amazon SageMaker AI's serverless model customization uses Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards to improve agentic tool calling, reducing hallucinations and errors in AI agents without infrastructure management.

articles.title: Building Intelligent Search with Amazon Bedrock and Amazon OpenSearch for hybrid RAG solutions
articles.summary: Agentic AI assistants powered by LLMs combine dynamic dialogue with real-time data retrieval through APIs and databases, known as Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), enabling intelligent task execution.

articles.title: Accelerating Vision AI Pipelines with Batch Mode VC-6 and NVIDIA Nsight
articles.summary: NVIDIA accelerates vision AI pipelines using batch mode VC-6 and Nsight tools to optimize decode, preprocessing, and GPU scheduling stages alongside improving model throughput.

articles.title: Bringing AI Closer to the Edge and On-Device with Gemma 4
articles.summary: Gemma 4 multimodal and multilingual models launched to scale from data centers to edge devices. Designed to meet growing demand for flexible AI deployment across the spectrum.

articles.title: Achieving Single-Digit Microsecond Latency Inference for Capital Markets
articles.summary: NVIDIA enables single-digit microsecond latency AI inference for algorithmic trading, helping firms compete in high-speed electronic markets using advanced neural network models instead of traditional hardware.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: A research study examines how generational differences shape consumer attitudes toward social media advertisements across various platforms in collaboration with CrowdDNA.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta commits to net zero emissions by 2030, developing carbon mitigation technology and sharing it openly to reduce its environmental impact.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: An individual with unconventional experience developed breakthrough extended reality technology. Their non-traditional path provided unique perspectives that drove innovation in XR.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Researchers developed methods to identify interactions at scale in Large Language Models to improve interpretability and transparency. This work aims to make LLM decision-making processes more understandable for safer, more trustworthy AI systems.

articles.title: Information-Driven Design of Imaging Systems
articles.summary: Research on information-driven design of imaging systems that uses an encoder to map objects to images while quantifying measurement quality despite noise corruption using only noisy data and noise models.

articles.title: RL without TD learning
articles.summary: A new RL algorithm using divide-and-conquer paradigm replaces temporal difference learning, addressing scalability challenges and performing better on long-horizon tasks.
